003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.*;
020import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.*;
021
022import java.lang.annotation.*;
023import java.math.*;
024import java.util.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
027
028/**
029 * Used to tailor how bean properties get marshalled by the framework.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * The marshalling-only sibling of {@link BeanProp @BeanProp}.
033 * Where {@code @BeanProp} (in <c>juneau-commons</c>) carries the bean-modeling attributes
034 * (such as {@code name}, {@code ro}, {@code wo}, {@code type}, {@code params}, {@code elementType},
035 * and {@code factory}), this annotation carries the wire-format-specific attributes used by serializers
036 * and parsers: a per-property format string and a bean dictionary for polymorphic types.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * Can be used in the following locations:
040 * <ul>
041 *    <li>Methods/Fields - Bean getters/setters and properties.
042 *    <li><ja>@Rest</ja>-annotated classes and <ja>@RestOp</ja>-annotated methods when used with {@link MarshalledPropApply @MarshalledPropApply}.
043 * </ul>
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
046 *    <li class='ja'>{@link BeanProp}
047 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MarshalledPropAnnotation">@MarshalledProp Annotation</a>
048 * </ul>
049 */
050@Documented
051@Target({ FIELD, METHOD, PARAMETER })
052@Retention(RUNTIME)
053@Inherited
054public @interface MarshalledProp {
055
056   /**
057    * Optional description for the exposed API.
058    *
059    * @return The annotation value.
060    * @since 9.2.0
061    */
062   String[] description() default {};
063
064   /**
065    * Declares the named views this property belongs to.
066    *
067    * <p>
068    * When an active view is selected (via
069    * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContext.Builder#activeView(String)} or the per-call session
070    * override), this property is included only when its declared view set contains the active view name.
071    *
072    * <p>
073    * If this member is empty (the default), the property follows the default-view-inclusion policy:
074    * by default an untagged property is included under every active view (matching Jackson's
075    * {@code DEFAULT_VIEW_INCLUSION} behavior). The policy can be flipped via
076    * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContext.Builder#disableDefaultViewInclusion()}.
077    *
078    * <p>
079    * Multiple view names are supported — a property tagged {@code view={"summary","detail"}} is included
080    * when <em>either</em> {@code "summary"} or {@code "detail"} is the active view (union semantics).
081    *
082    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
083    * <p class='bjava'>
084    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
085    *
086    *       <jc>// Included in all views (untagged = default inclusion)</jc>
087    *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>id</jf>;
088    *
089    *       <jc>// Included only in the "summary" and "detail" views</jc>
090    *       <ja>@MarshalledProp</ja>(view={<js>"summary"</js>, <js>"detail"</js>})
091    *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>name</jf>;
092    *
093    *       <jc>// Included only in the "detail" view</jc>
094    *       <ja>@MarshalledProp</ja>(view=<js>"detail"</js>)
095    *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>description</jf>;
096    *    }
097    * </p>
098    *
099    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
100    *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContext.Builder#activeView(String)}
101    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ViewProjection">View-based Projection</a>
102    * </ul>
103    *
104    * @return The annotation value.
105    * @since 10.0.0
106    */
107   String[] view() default {};
108
109   /**
110    * Bean dictionary.
111    *
112    * <p>
113    * The list of classes that make up the bean dictionary for this bean property, used during
114    * polymorphic marshalling.
115    *
116    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
117    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#dictionary()}
118    *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#dictionary()}
119    *    <li class='ja'>{@link MarshalledConfig#dictionary_replace()}
120    *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContext.Builder#beanDictionary(Class...)}
121    * </ul>
122    *
123    * <p>
124    * This annotation can also be used on private fields of a property.
125    *
126    * @return The annotation value.
127    */
128   Class<?>[] dictionary() default {};
129
130   /**
131    * Calendar wire format override.
132    *
133    * @return The annotation value.
134    */
135   CalendarFormat calendarFormat() default CalendarFormat.NOT_SET;
136
137   /**
138    * Date wire format override.
139    *
140    * @return The annotation value.
141    */
142   DateFormat dateFormat() default DateFormat.NOT_SET;
143
144   /**
145    * Duration wire format override.
146    *
147    * @return The annotation value.
148    */
149   DurationFormat durationFormat() default DurationFormat.NOT_SET;
150
151   /**
152    * Specifies a String format for converting the bean property value to a formatted string.
153    *
154    * <p>
155    * Note that this is usually a one-way conversion during serialization.
156    *
157    * <p>
158    * During parsing, we will attempt to convert the value to the original form by using the
159    * {@link MarshallingSession#convertToType(Object, Class)} but there is no guarantee that this will succeed.
160    *
161    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
162    * <p class='bjava'>
163    *    <ja>@MarshalledProp</ja>(format=<js>"$%.2f"</js>)
164    *    <jk>public float</jk> <jf>price</jf>;
165    * </p>
166    *
167    * <p>
168    * This annotation can also be used on private fields of a property like so:
169    *
170    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
171    * <p class='bjava'>
172    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
173    *
174    *       <ja>@MarshalledProp</ja>(format=<js>"$%.2f"</js>)
175    *       <jk>private float</jk> <jf>price</jf>;
176    *
177    *       <jk>public float</jk> getPrice() {
178    *          <jk>return</jk> <jf>price</jf>;
179    *       }
180    *    }
181    * </p>
182    *
183    * @return The annotation value.
184    */
185   String format() default "";
186
187   /**
188    * Locale wire format override.
189    *
190    * @return The annotation value.
191    */
192   LocaleFormat localeFormat() default LocaleFormat.NOT_SET;
193
194   /**
195    * Period wire format override.
196    *
197    * @return The annotation value.
198    */
199   PeriodFormat periodFormat() default PeriodFormat.NOT_SET;
200
201   /**
202    * Temporal wire format override.
203    *
204    * @return The annotation value.
205    */
206   TemporalFormat temporalFormat() default TemporalFormat.NOT_SET;
207
208   /**
209    * Time-zone wire format override.
210    *
211    * @return The annotation value.
212    */
213   TimeZoneFormat timeZoneFormat() default TimeZoneFormat.NOT_SET;
214
215   /**
216    * Binary wire format override for <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code> property values.
217    *
218    * @return The annotation value.
219    */
220   BinaryFormat binaryFormat() default BinaryFormat.NOT_SET;
221
222   /**
223    * Enum wire format override for {@link Enum} property values.
224    *
225    * @return The annotation value.
226    */
227   EnumFormat enumFormat() default EnumFormat.NOT_SET;
228
229   /**
230    * UUID wire format override for {@link UUID} property values.
231    *
232    * @return The annotation value.
233    */
234   UuidFormat uuidFormat() default UuidFormat.NOT_SET;
235
236   /**
237    * BitSet wire format override for {@link BitSet} property values.
238    *
239    * @return The annotation value.
240    */
241   BitSetFormat bitSetFormat() default BitSetFormat.NOT_SET;
242
243   /**
244    * Big-number wire format override for {@link BigInteger} / {@link BigDecimal} property values.
245    *
246    * @return The annotation value.
247    */
248   BigNumberFormat bigNumberFormat() default BigNumberFormat.NOT_SET;
249
250   /**
251    * Boolean wire format override for {@link Boolean} / <code><jk>boolean</jk></code> property values.
252    *
253    * @return The annotation value.
254    */
255   BooleanFormat booleanFormat() default BooleanFormat.NOT_SET;
256
257   /**
258    * Float / Double non-finite wire format override for {@link Float} / {@link Double} property values.
259    *
260    * @return The annotation value.
261    */
262   FloatFormat floatFormat() default FloatFormat.NOT_SET;
263
264   /**
265    * Currency wire format override for {@link Currency} property values.
266    *
267    * @return The annotation value.
268    */
269   CurrencyFormat currencyFormat() default CurrencyFormat.NOT_SET;
270
271   /**
272    * Class wire format override for {@link Class} property values.
273    *
274    * @return The annotation value.
275    */
276   ClassFormat classFormat() default ClassFormat.NOT_SET;
277
278   /**
279    * Per-property null-coercion policy applied when a {@code null} JSON-equivalent value reaches this property during
280    * parsing.
281    *
282    * <p>
283    * Juneau's analog of Jackson's {@code @JsonSetter(nulls=…)}.  When the parser encounters an explicit {@code null}
284    * (or an absent {@link Optional}) for this property, the configured policy decides whether to:
285    * <ul>
286    *    <li>{@link Nulls#LEAVE LEAVE} (default) — set the property to {@code null} (or, for an {@link Optional}-typed
287    *       property, to {@link Optional#empty()}).
288    *    <li>{@link Nulls#EMPTY EMPTY} — substitute the type's "empty" value (empty {@link String}/{@link java.util.Collection}/
289    *       {@link java.util.Map}, primitive default for primitives, {@code Optional.empty()} for {@link Optional}).
290    *    <li>{@link Nulls#DEFAULT DEFAULT} — substitute the bean-constructed default for the property (i.e. the value the
291    *       property holds on a fresh no-arg-constructed instance of the bean).  When no reference instance can be built,
292    *       falls back to {@link Nulls#LEAVE LEAVE}.
293    *    <li>{@link Nulls#SKIP SKIP} — do not call the setter at all, leaving any pre-existing value in place.
294    * </ul>
295    *
296    * <p>
297    * For an {@link Optional}-typed property, {@code EMPTY}/{@code DEFAULT} resolve to {@link Optional#empty()} —
298    * never a bare {@code null} inside an {@link Optional}.  The same contract applies to {@link java.util.OptionalInt},
299    * {@link java.util.OptionalLong}, and {@link java.util.OptionalDouble}.
300    *
301    * <p>
302    * When this member is {@link Nulls#NOT_SET NOT_SET} (the default), the context-level default configured on
303    * {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.Parser.Builder#nulls(Nulls)} applies.
304    *
305    * @return The annotation value.
306    * @since 10.0.0
307    */
308   Nulls nulls() default Nulls.NOT_SET;
309}
